summary: GitHub Copilot responded normally without any blocking or warning. The prompt 'Hello, how are you?' was sent in normal (logged-in) mode and Copilot replied with a greeting and list of capabilities. No Etap warning text was injected into the response. The is_http2=2 + END_STREAM=false + SSE \n fix does not appear to trigger warning delivery for this service.

details.response_summary: Copilot greeted back and listed its capabilities (code search, issues/PRs, reading repo data, writing files, debugging, GitHub product questions).
details.page_title: Introduction to GitHub Copilot · GitHub Copilot

notes: Previous test #007 had Thinking infinite wait issue, #022 was blocked by login requirement in incognito. This test in normal mode confirms Copilot works but Etap warning is NOT being delivered. Possible causes: (1) Copilot traffic not matching APF rule, (2) H2 connection reuse bypassing Etap, (3) Warning injection not configured for this service yet.
